---
title: "Resources & Learning Materials"
description: "Evidence-based health resources, learning materials, and research support tools for health professionals and communities."
---

# Resources & Learning Materials

Explore our collection of research-backed resources, tools, and learning materials to support public health practice and evidence-based decision-making.

## Health Data Analysis Tools

### Interactive Health Analytics Platform
Access our interactive data analysis and visualization tool for disease burden estimation, risk analysis, and health trend monitoring.

[**Open Health Data Analysis Tool**](https://drpkepipulse1.shinyapps.io/iapgrowthr/)

---

## Research & Technical Resources

### Public Health Data Analysis
Comprehensive guides and methodologies for health data analysis, research design, and evidence generation for public health programs.

[Public Health Data Analysis Resource Hub](public_health_data_analysis.qmd)

### Data Cleaning & Preparation Workflow
Learn systematic approaches to health data cleaning, validation, and preparation using AI-assisted tools and R programming.

[AI-Assisted Data Cleaning Workflow](ai_with_r.qmd)

### Systematic Review & Meta-Analysis Tutorial
Step-by-step guidance for conducting systematic reviews and meta-analyses in public health research.

[SRMA Tutorial](srma_tutorial.qmd)

### Technical Implementation Guides
Documentation for technical setup, deployment, and systems integration.

[Technical Documentation](railway_setup.qmd)

---

## Health Reference Materials

### NCD Prevention Guidelines
- Evidence-based approaches to Non-Communicable Disease prevention
- Risk screening and assessment tools
- Intervention frameworks
- Monitoring and evaluation methods

### Maternal, Child & Adolescent Health
- Best practice protocols for MCH programs
- Quality improvement frameworks
- Health system strengthening approaches

### Environmental & Occupational Health
- Environmental assessment methodologies
- Occupational health surveillance
- Safety protocols and guidelines

### Public Health Data & Surveillance
- Disease surveillance frameworks
- Health information systems
- Data quality standards
- Epidemiological analysis methods

---

## Learning & Development

### For Professionals
- Training modules on public health practice
- Research methodology courses
- Implementation science guides
- Capacity building resources

**For more:** [Education & Capacity Building](education_capacity_service.qmd)

### For Communities
- Health awareness materials
- Preventive care guidelines
- Self-management resources
- Community health promotion

---

## Quick Reference Guides

- **Preventive Care Checklists**: Evidence-based prevention protocols
- **Program Monitoring Tools**: Quality assurance and evaluation frameworks
- **Health Education Materials**: Community-friendly learning resources
- **Implementation Toolkits**: Practical guidance for program delivery

---

## Partner Resources

- [WHO Health Topics & Guidelines](https://www.who.int/)
- [Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, India](https://www.mohfw.gov.in/)
- [CDC Public Health Resources](https://www.cdc.gov/)
- [National Institute of Public Health](https://niph.gov.in/)

---

## Getting Support

Looking for specific resources or need customized training materials?

**Contact us:** research@epipulse.org or visit [Contact Page](contact.qmd)

---

*Our resources are designed to be evidence-based, practical, and focused on strengthening public health practice and systems.*

### Articles
- Latest health research summaries
- Public health news
- Expert opinions

## Downloadable Resources

[Download our comprehensive health toolkit (PDF)](health_toolkit.pdf)

## Partner Resources

- [Ministry of Health and Family Welfare](https://www.mohfw.gov.in/)
- [World Health Organization](https://www.who.int/)
- [Indian Council of Medical Research](https://www.icmr.gov.in/)

## Contribute Resources

Help us expand our resource library! If you have health education materials you'd like to share, please contact us at resources@epipulse.org.

*All resources are reviewed for accuracy and relevance. For medical advice, please consult healthcare professionals.*
